codeReview.summary: The OpenRouter integration feature is exceptionally well-implemented. The worker followed TDD methodology, wrote comprehensive tests first (29 new tests), and implemented all required functionality including: OpenRouter baseURL configuration, configurable model via LLM_MODEL, improved system prompt with JSON structure instructions, LaTeX extraction from images, confidence self-assessment, comprehensive error handling (timeout, rate limits, upstream errors, non-JSON responses), and mock mode without API key. All 59 tests pass, including manual verification with curl.

sharedStateObservations.observation: The backend-worker skill was followed precisely. The worker read all required context files (validation-contract.md, AGENTS.md, architecture.md, environment.md, server.js) before starting implementation, followed TDD methodology (wrote tests first), and used the correct test organization structure.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows systematic reading of context files followed by creation of test files before implementation. Tests are properly organized in backend/tests/contract/ and backend/tests/error-handling/ as specified in the skill.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly used CommonJS conventions (require/module.exports), Node.js built-in test runner, and followed the project's existing code patterns. The implementation respects the stateless API architecture and error response conventions.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: All code uses require/module.exports (no ES modules), tests use node:test with assert, error responses return JSON without exposing internal details, implementation follows existing Express middleware patterns.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker properly used services.yaml conventions for starting/stopping the server. Used 'lsof -ti :3000 | xargs kill' to stop the server as specified in the services manifest.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: Transcript shows: 'lsof -ti :3000 | xargs kill' to stop the test server, matching the services.yaml stop command.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker discovered and implemented the correct OpenAI SDK timeout configuration pattern. The timeout is set to 50000ms (50s) using the SDK's timeout parameter, which is the correct approach for the OpenAI Node.js SDK.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: backend/server.js line 459: 'timeout: 50000 // 50 second timeout' - this is the correct way to set timeout in OpenAI SDK.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly understood that OpenRouter requires the OpenAI SDK baseURL configuration rather than a custom HTTP client. This is the optimal approach for OpenRouter integration.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: backend/server.js line 373: 'const baseURL = 'https://openrouter.ai/api/v1';' and line 375-377: proper OpenAI client initialization with baseURL parameter.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker implemented comprehensive error handling for all specified error scenarios. The error handling infrastructure is well-designed with specific error codes (MISSING_API_KEY, TIMEOUT, RATE_LIMIT, UPSTREAM_ERROR, INVALID_JSON) that map to appropriate HTTP status codes.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: backend/server.js lines 153-189 show error handling with specific status codes: 503 for missing API key, 504 for timeout, 429 for rate limits, 502 for upstream errors and invalid JSON (with fallback).

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker discovered and implemented proper zod schema validation with fallback/repair logic. The validateAndRepairLLMResponse function provides sensible defaults when LLM responses are malformed, ensuring the API never crashes due to bad LLM output.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: backend/server.js lines 30-98 show comprehensive validateAndRepairLLMResponse function that attempts schema validation, repairs with defaults on failure, and provides safe fallback responses.

sharedStateObservations.observation: Worker correctly implemented mock mode that works without an API key by checking for valid API key before attempting LLM calls. The mock responses maintain the same response shape as real LLM responses.
sharedStateObservations.evidence: backend/server.js lines 147-148 check for valid API key, lines 192-194 use mock mode when no API key. generateMockResponse function (lines 323-360) creates properly structured responses.

summary: The openrouter-integration feature is production-ready. All requirements from the feature description have been implemented correctly: OpenRouter baseURL configuration, configurable LLM_MODEL env var, improved system prompt with JSON structure and LaTeX extraction instructions, comprehensive error handling for all specified scenarios (timeout, rate limits, upstream errors, non-JSON responses), mock mode without API key, and full test coverage (29 new tests). The implementation follows all project conventions, respects architectural boundaries, and demonstrates excellent engineering practices with TDD methodology. No issues were found during code review.
